appliedUpdates.description: Updated .factory/library/index-compression.md to note that format version 2 was subsequently bumped to 3 by index-incremental-updates, preventing confusion for future workers.

Created .factory/library/index-regex-prefilter.md documenting the regex prefiltering strategy: which patterns are safe for index acceleration, key constants (MAX_REGEX_CLASS_LITERALS=10, MAX_REGEX_PREFILTER_LITERALS=64), and the FullScan fallback behavior.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update backend-worker skill step 4 (benchmark timing) to acknowledge time.perf_counter() as a valid alternative to hyperfine when structured per-invocation timing is needed for JSON output (e.g., GPU benchmarks with per-device rows).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: benchmarks/run_gpu_benchmarks.py uses time.perf_counter() because it needs per-device structured JSON that hyperfine cannot produce. The current skill says 'Uses hyperfine or Measure-Command for timing (not Python time.time)' which doesn't cover this case.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note in the Architecture/Index section about the soft-delete file_id management pattern: deleted file entries remain in the files vec with deleted=true, file_ids are never reclaimed, and the file_trigrams reverse-map is rebuilt from postings on load. This is a significant design choice that future workers modifying the index should understand.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: index-incremental-updates (commit 8a004db) introduced soft-delete. Worker documented it in .factory/library/index-incremental-updates.md but it's an architectural decision that belongs in AGENTS.md's key invariants or a dedicated index architecture section.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the rust-worker skill step 2 (TDD: write a failing Rust test first) to allow Python failing-first tests for features that are Python benchmark tooling, not Rust code. Two separate worker sessions (50aef367 and 40dcd51f) for index-scaling-validation both reported the same deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: index-scaling-validation workers both deviated from 'write a failing Rust test' because the feature was Python benchmark script work. skillFeedback.deviations[0] from both sessions: 'I wrote failing Python unit tests in tests/unit/test_benchmark_scripts.py before changing the benchmark script.'

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker correctly documented TG_SIDECAR_TIMEOUT_MS in python-sidecar-ipc.md (gpu-error-hardening)
Narrowed exception catch from except Exception to except ConfigurationError for pipeline creation in sidecar.py (gpu-error-hardening)
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — implementation-specific choice, not a systemic convention gap; non-ConfigurationError failures during pipeline creation are rare edge cases
duplicate — version changes too frequently to track in AGENTS.md; library notes already document version per feature

rejectedObservations.observation: Backward-compatible 'matches' key alongside 'indexed_matches' in benchmark payload (index-scaling-validation)
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — minor implementation detail, not a systemic convention; insufficient evidence of cross-worker pattern
